pattern: Greedy two-pointer on sorted tokens: spend the cheapest token for a point when you can afford it, sell the most expensive for power when you cannot.
hints: Sorting the tokens allows a greedy strategy: the cheapest token is the best to play face-up (costs least power), and the most expensive is the best to sell face-down (gains most power).
Maintain a left pointer (cheapest) and right pointer (most expensive); greedily play face-up from the left when possible, and face-down from the right when score > 0.
Track the maximum score seen at any point, since you might want to stop before exhausting all tokens.

edge_inputs.description: can chain face-down then face-up to increase score
maximum power and maximum single token
score=0 initially so face-down is impossible at start
best strategy involves selling most expensive token to unlock cheaper ones
all tokens same value, at boundary of affordability

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(1000, max(0, size))
    tokens = [rng.randint(0, 9999) for _ in range(n)]
    power = rng.randint(0, 9999)
    return [tokens, power]
